name: Fear Gorta
description: The Fear Gorta, meaning 'Hungry Man' or 'Man of Hunger,' is a spectral creature in Irish folklore. It is often depicted as a gaunt, emaciated beggar or famine victim, appearing in places where poverty and hunger were prevalent. Unlike other malevolent spirits, the Fear Gorta is not inherently evil. It is said that if one shows compassion and gives alms to the Fear Gorta, they will be rewarded with abundance, often with a pot of gold or an unending supply of food. However, ignoring or mistreating the Fear Gorta can bring misfortune and prolonged hunger to the household. Some accounts suggest the Fear Gorta is a restless spirit, forever searching for sustenance it was denied in life, while others believe it is a manifestation of the collective suffering of the starving during times of famine. The appearance of the Fear Gorta serves as a potent reminder of the importance of charity and empathy, highlighting the devastating consequences of neglect and indifference to the plight of the less fortunate. It embodies both the desperation of hunger and the potential for generosity to alleviate suffering.
subtitle: The Hungry Man, Beggar of Plenty
appearance: The Fear Gorta typically appears as a skeletal or emaciated figure, often dressed in rags or tattered clothing. Its skin is pale and drawn, with sunken eyes that reflect the emptiness of hunger. It may carry a begging bowl or a staff, and its presence is often accompanied by a chilling wind or a sense of despair.
habitat: The Fear Gorta is most often encountered in areas associated with poverty, famine, or hardship, such as abandoned fields, deserted villages, or along lonely roads. It may also appear near homes of those who are wealthy but ungenerous.
behavior: The Fear Gorta approaches people and begs for food or alms. Its behavior is generally passive, but its presence is unsettling and evokes feelings of pity and unease. It tests the compassion of those it encounters, rewarding generosity with abundance and punishing stinginess with misfortune.

keyMyths.title: The Beggar and the Golden Pot
keyMyths.description: A kind farmer, despite his own meager resources, shared his food with a Fear Gorta. In return, the farmer found a pot of gold in his field, ensuring his family's prosperity for generations. This emphasizes the reward for generosity.

sources.author: Lady Wilde

sources.description: A collection of Irish folklore and superstitions, including accounts of the Fear Gorta.
